executive_summary: Critical vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiClient EMS and F5 BIG-IP are being actively exploited in the wild right now. The European Commission suffered its second data breach this year, with hackers claiming to steal sensitive data. Russian state-sponsored group Star Blizzard (TA446) is deploying DarkSword exploit kit to target iPhones in phishing campaigns. Supply chain attacks continue with TeamPCP compromising Telnyx SDK on PyPI to steal cloud credentials. Iran-linked hackers claim a major breach of Lockheed Martin and attacked FBI Director Kash Patel's email.

items.name: Star Blizzard (TA446)

items.targets: Telnyx SDK users, Databricks platform customers, cloud developers
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1554 (Compromise Client Software Binary) - Backdoored Python SDK on PyPI for credential theft

items.name: Unknown threat actor (impersonating CERT-UA)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1036.005 (Match Legitimate Name or Location), T1219 (Remote Access Software) - AgeWheeze RAT deployment via phishing

canada_landscape.summary: Two articles reference Canada, both relating to smart home security gaps in post-breach guidance. No CCCS advisories were identified in this 48-hour period. No breaches or incidents involving major Canadian retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were reported.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added two critical vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog that are being actively exploited in the wild: CVE-2026-33017 (Langflow RCE) and CVE-2026-33634 (Aquasecurity Trivy supply chain flaw), both exploited within hours of disclosure. The TeamPCP threat group has compromised the Telnyx PyPI package (742,000 downloads) in an ongoing supply chain attack targeting AI developers. Iran-linked Handala hackers claim to have breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email account. A Canadian company, GeoMechanics Technologies, was listed as a victim of the Akira ransomware group. Dutch Police disclosed a security breach after falling victim to a phishing attack, highlighting that even law enforcement is not immune to social engineering.

canada_landscape.summary: One Canadian company, GeoMechanics Technologies, was publicly listed as a victim of the Akira ransomware group this period. No CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period. Multiple articles referenced Canada in general cybersecurity context including quantum cryptography discussions and international trade agreements, but no specific incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were reported.
